title: Emergentist Compass External Calibration Claims
authorityBoundary: Machine-readable calibration records only. The Markdown ledger owns semantic wording. This file cannot create doctrine, validate the whole Compass, or promote evidence tiers.

wholeCompassVerdict.wording: No whole-system calibration stage is assigned. Components range from X0 to X1 or not applicable; prospective discrimination and independent replication remain pending.

claims.title: Present representations of possible futures can reweight present action
claims.compassClaim: A present D4 model token representing D5 possible content can alter present action weights through M star A.
claims.currentVerdict: External task-bounded cue, association, representation, and circuit-intervention findings anchor the crosswalk; the exact Compass edge and Soul Loop advantage remain untested.
claims.intervention: Randomize present future cues or perturb a preregistered prospective-planning mechanism while holding current rewards and available actions fixed as far as possible.
claims.prediction: Future-model intervention changes present choice in the preregistered direction and adds held-out prediction beyond named rivals.
killCriteria: matched future-cue or circuit perturbation leaves present choice unchanged
prospective-planning perturbation does not impair model-based choice
a simpler rival predicts as well or better

dataset.access: published studies and identified repositories; no Compass analysis claimed

claims.scopeBoundary: No realized future state or signal is claimed to cause the present action backward in physical time.

claims.title: Agent-relative optionality is measurable but not one universal scalar objective
claims.compassClaim: An option cone is a typed agent-relative subset of physically admissible histories whose metrics may predict bounded action and competence.
claims.currentVerdict: Empowerment, POWER, reachability, path entropy, and viability are established distinct constructs; universal option maximization is rejected.
claims.intervention: Decorrelate optionality metrics from reward, risk, information, and cost; vary embodiment and other-agent effects.
claims.prediction: A typed option-cone vector predicts bounded competence better than any single optionality scalar in the declared task class.
killCriteria: no option metric adds prediction beyond reward and risk
the typed vector performs worse than a simpler scalar
the apparent gain is a representation or duplicate-counting artifact
claims.scopeBoundary: The physical light cone and c are unchanged.

claims.title: The product aggregator competes with other conjunctive forms
claims.compassClaim: In a bounded task class, the product of operationalized Phi and V predicts action success better than prespecified alternative aggregators.
claims.currentVerdict: The product has mathematical and bargaining precedents but no universal external fit validation.
claims.intervention: Evaluate identical folds and features across product, minimum, harmonic, additive, CES, Cobb-Douglas, Nash-surplus, and maximin models.
claims.prediction: The product wins prespecified held-out metrics in the bounded task class without post-hoc rescaling.
killCriteria: a rival consistently wins
the zero-factor boundary fails
Phi or V cannot be measured with acceptable reliability
claims.scopeBoundary: Nash's product neither derives the Compass variables nor protects omitted third parties.

claims.title: Macrovariables can constrain or reweight lower-level dynamics
claims.compassClaim: Higher-level organization may restrict or reweight lower-law-admissible trajectories without adding forbidden ones.
claims.currentVerdict: Externally anchored as domain-relative constraint, coarse-graining, and causal-emergence work; no universal macro superiority.
claims.intervention: Perturb realizations of the macrovariable under matched microstate distributions and controlled exogenous conditions; do not condition on an identical complete microstate while claiming an independent macro intervention.
claims.prediction: The macrovariable adds prediction or intervention value robustly across reasonable grains while native-unit costs remain within preregistered component budgets; any scalar penalty is unit-valid.
killCriteria: gain disappears under fair micro or environment controls
result depends on one arbitrary partition
controller or thermodynamic costs are hidden or dimensionally laundered
real intervention fails

dataset.access: Vicsek, ecology, and evolution exemplars identified

claims.scopeBoundary: Predictive and interventional emergence must be distinguished; neither implies a new force or metaphysical strong emergence.

claims.title: Mu-crossings are candidate local regime transitions
claims.compassClaim: A mu-crossing marks saturation, a newly useful effective freedom or boundary result, lower-level recovery, prediction, and kill criterion.
claims.currentVerdict: Phase, ecological, and evolutionary transitions are externally real; exactly six universal crossings and literal new microscopic dimensions are unsupported.
claims.intervention: Apply one frozen crossing protocol to transition and smooth non-transition controls in multiple domains.
claims.prediction: The frozen protocol discriminates unseen transition from non-transition cases without domain-specific relabeling.
killCriteria: no replicated change point
smooth crossover wins
novelty vanishes under complexity penalty
mapping requires post-hoc labels

dataset.access: published physics, ecology, and evolution sources; cognition remains a prospective domain

claims.scopeBoundary: A successful reduction reclassifies a crossing as reduced; missing reduction is never evidence of irreducibility.

claims.title: Egregoreotype is a composite trace-and-selection candidate
claims.compassClaim: A candidate requires persistent trace, carrier turnover, causal selection reweighting, recurrent objective-like bias, and visible substrate costs.
claims.currentVerdict: Several components have separate external anchors; the five-condition composite, recurrent objective-like kind, and visible-cost criterion are not independently validated.
claims.intervention: Cross fixed versus replaced carriers with trace preservation, erasure, scrambling, and counterfeiting; reverse incentives and vary centralized instruction.
claims.prediction: The composite variable adds held-out prediction beyond all prespecified simpler mechanisms and survives full carrier replacement.
killCriteria: trace intervention has no effect
turnover destroys the pattern
no recurrent direction survives
costs cannot be assigned
a simpler mechanism predicts equally well
claims.scopeBoundary: Candidacy does not imply consciousness, personhood, sovereignty, goodness, or literal energy feeding.

claims.title: Models, participation, and receipts form bounded reflexive feedback
claims.compassClaim: A fallible model affects action, action affects conditions, and receipts can update both model and selector.
claims.currentVerdict: Conceptually attributed to Soros and independently anchored by controlled expectations-feedback and asset-market experiments.
claims.intervention: Randomize forecast information or feedback sign/gain and measure belief-action-outcome-update paths.
claims.prediction: Declared feedback sign and model intervention improve held-out prediction of action and receipt dynamics.
killCriteria: model intervention does not change action
feedback sign adds no prediction
experience or fundamentals fully explain the effect
claims.scopeBoundary: Feedback is ordinary forward causation; its positive or negative sign is dynamical, not moral.

claims.title: Syntropic Dyadism is a declared Justice rule
claims.compassClaim: Strict syntropy requires durable positive deltas for part and whole under a bearer-complete Justice envelope.
claims.currentVerdict: Normative construction, not an empirical discovery or derivation from the chart; empirical consequence claims use a separate card.
claims.intervention: Apply a bearer-completeness and logical-counterexample audit to the proposed classification; no experiment is treated as confirmation of moral truth.
outcomes: voluntary sacrifice remains a distinct class
claims.prediction: Inside the accepted Justice axiom, strict syntropy rejects any classification that hides a materially harmed bearer or relabels voluntary sacrifice as mutual gain.
killCriteria: the predicates are internally contradictory
a materially affected bearer can be omitted without changing the verdict
the moral rule is claimed to follow from the reciprocal chart or natural selection
claims.scopeBoundary: Empirical results cannot prove the moral truth of the Justice axiom.

claims.title: Justice-envelope institutions have separately testable consequences
claims.compassClaim: In a bounded institutional setting, bearer-complete Justice controls improve prespecified harm detection, correction, durability, or exit outcomes over named governance rivals.
claims.currentVerdict: Social-choice and cooperation experiments anchor the relevant tradeoffs; the complete Justice envelope has not been externally tested as one intervention.
claims.intervention: Compare prespecified institutions with and without named Justice controls while retaining all bearer-level receipts and equalizing non-treatment resources.
claims.prediction: Justice controls improve at least one preregistered institutional outcome without worsening an omitted bearer because omission is itself a failed run.
killCriteria: the complete envelope adds no consequence beyond a simpler rival
bearers or costs cannot be measured
aggregate gains erase a harmed bearer
cooperation or punishment is relabeled as strict syntropy
claims.scopeBoundary: A consequence result moves only this empirical card; it neither confirms Syntropic Dyadism nor proves moral realism.

claims.title: S2 is a selected host, not an externally calibrated territory
claims.compassClaim: For a prespecified observable domain, S2 is the latent geometry of the data and predicts held-out observations better than named alternative geometries.
claims.currentVerdict: No direct external calibration supports S2 as the geometry of reality, awareness, agency, or ethics.
claims.intervention: Compare S2 with Euclidean, hyperbolic, toroidal, simplex, and flexible latent alternatives under identical preprocessing and penalties.
claims.prediction: S2 uniquely and robustly outperforms prespecified alternative geometries on an independently selected observable domain.
killCriteria: another geometry fits equally well or better
the inferred geometry is unstable
the observables do not identify the host
claims.scopeBoundary: A perceptual bound could calibrate a perceptual model only; it cannot validate the geometry of reality.

claims.title: The 4+3 Rosetta route is an Agentz dispatch grammar
claims.compassClaim: Varna to Pramana to reasoning to discipline to regime to equation defines the semantic skeleton of four deployable L1-L4 roles, three L5-L7 boundary-witness passes, and a return loop.
claims.currentVerdict: Structurally declared inside the framework; no external result yet shows that this exact 4+3 routing improves agent performance.
claims.intervention: Randomly assign matched tasks to the 4+3 Rosetta route, a flat single-agent baseline, and prespecified alternative routing pipelines under equal budget.
claims.prediction: The frozen Rosetta routing policy improves quality and correction per unit cost on prespecified heterogeneous tasks without post-hoc role reassignment.
killCriteria: a flat or shorter rival performs equally well or better
benefit disappears after equalizing compute and prompt-development budget
row assignments require post-hoc relabeling
claims.scopeBoundary: The cascade is a semantic skeleton until typed interfaces, dispatch predicates, handoffs, loops, stops, permissions, budgets, and evaluation complete a workflow; intervention is required for any causal-performance claim.

claims.title: Titan, closure, Rosetta, and quantum correspondences are non-calibrating
claims.compassClaim: Selected symbolic and cross-domain correspondences can compress a topology while preserving types and tiers.
claims.currentVerdict: Interpretive correspondence only; no empirical or ontological tier transfer is licensed.
claims.intervention: Remove every optional correspondence and verify that the operational calculus remains executable.
claims.prediction: Removal leaves commitment, receipt, feedback, constraint, and composition rules intact.
killCriteria: an operational rule depends on a correspondence
evidence is promoted through analogy
arithmetic or physics is changed to preserve the emblem
claims.scopeBoundary: Neither Everett nor Copenhagen is an extra dimension, and neither mu nor chi is quantum measurement.

sourceNegativeChecks.pattern: future state physically causes the present
This is a claim about the geometry of reality
